# Research 10: Transposition — Gauge Group from Three Primes

*Thread 3g.1 of sub-phase 3.B: transpose Paper 11's Theorem 11.5 (the*
*Standard Model gauge group SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1)/Z_6 from three-qubit*
*entanglement) into the Bost–Connes setting as a statement about the*
*smallest non-trivial Hecke algebra, generated by the three primes*
*{2, 3, 5}.*

*Authors: G Six, with Claude Opus 4.6*
*Date opened: 2026-04-09*
*Sub-phase 3.B, thread 3g.1 of `paper12/03-phase-3-plan.md` §1.2.*
*Depends on: research/04 (Identity 12 rigorous), paper11 Theorem 11.5,*
*paper11/research/07 (A_2 from SLOCC orbit).*

---

## 0. One-paragraph summary

Paper 11 proves that the Standard Model gauge group
G_SM = SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1)/Z_6 emerges as the stabiliser structure
of the GHZ tangent space in (C²)^⊗3, with the A_2 root system carried
on the tangent space of the three-qubit SLOCC orbit and the Z_6
quotient coming from the (Z_2)² discrete stabiliser of |GHZ⟩ combined
with the Z_3 index of the A_2 root lattice inside its weight lattice.
This note transposes that theorem, via Identity 12 (research/04),
into the Bost–Connes setting. Under the unitary U : H_e → H_1, the
three "qubits" of Paper 11 become the three smallest primes {2, 3, 5},
the GHZ orbit becomes a specific orbit of the BC Hecke action at
those primes, and the A_2 root system emerges from the commutation
relations μ_p e(r) μ_p^* = (1/p) Σ_{ps=r} e(s) restricted to
p ∈ {2, 3, 5}. The conclusion: **SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1)/Z_6 is the
automorphism group of the smallest non-trivial sub-Hecke algebra of
the BC system generated by three primes.** What is rigorous here is
the identification of the sub-semigroup, its GNS orbit, and the
Z_2 × Z_3 discrete structure; what is structural (not yet a proof)
is the A_2 → su(3) step inside the BC setting; what is open is the
derivation of the continuous SU(3) factor from the BC commutation
relations (as opposed to reading it off from the Paper 11 tangent
space via transport along U).

---

## 1. What Paper 11 proves (the source theorem)

We restate Theorem 11.5 in the precise form given in
`paper11/research/07-paper-11-a2-root-system-from-slocc.md`.

### 1.1 Setup

Let H_q := (C²)^⊗3, with computational basis
{|000⟩, |001⟩, …, |111⟩}. Let

$$
|GHZ\rangle \;=\; \tfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\,\bigl(\,|000\rangle + |111\rangle\,\bigr)
\;\in\; H_q.
\tag{1.1}
$$

The SLOCC group is G = SL(2, C)^3, with Lie algebra
g = sl(2, C)^3. Write h_i, e_i, f_i for the standard sl(2)
generators in the i-th slot (i = 1, 2, 3).

### 1.2 Tangent space

The nine tangent vectors are computed in
`paper11/research/07`:

| Generator | Action on |GHZ⟩ |
|:----------|:------------------|
| h_i, i = 1, 2, 3 | (1/√2)(|000⟩ − |111⟩) (all three equal) |
| e_i | (1/√2) · (basis vector with single 1 in slot 3 − i; cyclic) |
| f_i | (1/√2) · (basis vector with single 1 in slot i; cyclic) |

The Cartan **collapses** to a single independent direction
(h_1|GHZ⟩ = h_2|GHZ⟩ = h_3|GHZ⟩), so the tangent space has
(complex) dimension 1 + 6 = 7.

### 1.3 Stabiliser

The continuous stabiliser is the two-dimensional torus

$$
\mathfrak{stab} \;=\; \{(a_1 h, a_2 h, a_3 h) : a_1 + a_2 + a_3 = 0\},
\tag{1.2}
$$

and the discrete stabiliser is

$$
Z_2 \times Z_2 \;=\; \{(\varepsilon_1 I, \varepsilon_2 I, \varepsilon_3 I) : \varepsilon_i = \pm 1,\;\varepsilon_1\varepsilon_2\varepsilon_3 = 1\}.
\tag{1.3}
$$

### 1.4 Root system

With T² parametrised by (a_1, a_2) and a_3 = −(a_1 + a_2), the six
non-zero weights on the tangent space are

$$
\Phi \;=\; \{(\pm 1, 0),\;(0, \pm 1),\;\pm(1, 1)\}.
\tag{1.4}
$$

Choosing simple roots α_1 = (1, 0), α_2 = (−1, 1), the Cartan matrix
is

$$
A \;=\; \begin{pmatrix}\phantom{-}2 & -1 \\ -1 & \phantom{-}2\end{pmatrix},
\tag{1.5}
$$

which is the A_2 Cartan matrix, i.e., su(3).

### 1.5 The Z_6 quotient

Z_6 = Z_2 × Z_3, where Z_2 is the centre of SU(2) supplied by the
discrete stabiliser (1.3), and Z_3 is the centre of SU(3) supplied
by the index [Λ_w : Λ_r] = 3 of the root lattice inside the weight
lattice of A_2.

### 1.6 Theorem 11.5 (source statement)

> **Theorem 11.5 (Paper 11).** *The stabiliser + tangent-space*
> *structure of the three-qubit GHZ orbit in (C²)^⊗3 / SL(2,C)^3*
> *carries an A_2 root system and a Z_2 × Z_3 discrete group, and*
> *the resulting real Lie group is*
>
> $$
>   G_{\mathrm{SM}} \;=\; \frac{SU(3) \times SU(2) \times U(1)}{Z_6}.
> $$

This is the statement we wish to transpose.

---

## 2. The BC-side setup

### 2.1 The three smallest primes as a sub-semigroup

Let P_3 := {2, 3, 5} ⊂ N*, the three smallest primes. They generate
a free abelian sub-semigroup

$$
\Sigma_3 \;:=\; \{2^{a} 3^{b} 5^{c} : a, b, c \in \mathbb{N}\} \;\subset\; \mathbb{N}^*.
\tag{2.1}
$$

Σ_3 is the smallest non-trivial multiplicatively-free sub-monoid
of N* containing three generators. (The two-prime sub-monoid
{2, 3} gives rank 2 and can only reach A_1 × A_1 or A_1; the single
prime gives rank 1 and can only give U(1); four primes gives rank
4 and is too large for the SM. **Three is the unique rank that
matches the SM rank.**)

### 2.2 The sub-Hecke algebra A_{Σ_3}

On the BC side (research/04 §3), the GNS Hilbert space H_1 at
β = 1 has orthonormal basis {μ_n Ω_1 : n ∈ N*}, with
H_BC (μ_n Ω_1) = log(n) · (μ_n Ω_1).

Define the **three-prime sub-Hecke algebra** as

$$
A_{\Sigma_3} \;:=\; C^*\bigl(\,\{\mu_p : p \in P_3\} \cup \{e(r) : r \in \mathbb{Q}_{\Sigma_3}/\mathbb{Z}\}\,\bigr)
\;\subset\; A_{\mathrm{BC}},
\tag{2.2}
$$

where Q_{Σ_3} ⊂ Q is the localisation of Z at {2, 3, 5}, and the
generators satisfy the restricted BC relations

$$
\mu_p \mu_q = \mu_{pq},
\quad
\mu_p^* \mu_p = 1,
\quad
\mu_p\,e(r)\,\mu_p^* \;=\; \frac{1}{p}\!\!\sum_{ps = r} e(s),
\quad p, q \in P_3.
\tag{2.3}
$$

The GNS sub-Hilbert space H_{1, Σ_3} ⊂ H_1 is the closed span of
{μ_n Ω_1 : n ∈ Σ_3}.

**Remark.** A_{Σ_3} is a genuine C*-subalgebra of A_BC; the
restrictions of the BC KMS state ω_1 to A_{Σ_3} and of H_BC to
H_{1, Σ_3} are consistent (the BC time evolution preserves the
Σ_3-sector because σ_t(μ_p) = p^{it} μ_p).

### 2.3 The three isometries μ_2, μ_3, μ_5

On the cosine-mode basis {|n⟩_e} of H_e (research/04 §2), under
the unitary U : H_e → H_1, μ_p corresponds to the dilation D_p:

$$
U\,D_p\,U^* \;=\; \mu_p,
\qquad
D_p \,|n\rangle_e \;=\; |p n\rangle_e,
\qquad p \in P_3.
\tag{2.4}
$$

So the three isometries {μ_2, μ_3, μ_5} act on {μ_n Ω_1} as
multiplication by 2, 3, 5 respectively in the N* index.

---

## 3. The transposition dictionary (three qubits ↔ three primes)

The core content of this note is the following table. Each row is
a Paper 11 object and its BC-side transpose; the rightmost column
records the current rigour status.

| Paper 11 object | BC-side transpose | Status |
|:----------------|:------------------|:-------|
| Qubit slot i ∈ {1, 2, 3} | Prime p_i ∈ {2, 3, 5} | rigorous (by fiat, matched via rank) |
| Hilbert space (C²)^⊗3, dim = 8 | Cyclic Σ_3-sub-GNS generated by Ω_1 up to "one move per prime": span{Ω_1, μ_2 Ω_1, μ_3 Ω_1, μ_5 Ω_1, μ_6 Ω_1, μ_{10} Ω_1, μ_{15} Ω_1, μ_{30} Ω_1}, 8 vectors | rigorous (explicit) |
| Computational basis |n_1 n_2 n_3⟩, n_i ∈ {0, 1} | μ_{2^{n_1} 3^{n_2} 5^{n_3}} Ω_1 with n_i ∈ {0, 1} (the "squarefree cube" in Σ_3) | rigorous (bijection) |
| GHZ state (1/√2)(|000⟩ + |111⟩) | Ψ_3 := (1/√2)(Ω_1 + μ_{30} Ω_1) | structural (motivated) |
| SLOCC group SL(2, C)^3 | Three commuting "Hecke rotations" generated by (μ_p, μ_p^*, e(r_p)) for p ∈ {2, 3, 5} | structural (see §5) |
| T² stabiliser (1.2) | Two-torus of BC phase rotations e(r) ∘ σ_t fixing Ψ_3 | structural |
| Z_2 × Z_2 discrete stabiliser | Sign flips on the three primes, product = +1 | rigorous |
| A_2 root system Φ | Spectral differences of H_BC inside Σ_3 at the "cube" level | structural (see §6) |
| Z_3 = Λ_w / Λ_r (triality) | Σ_3 / (Σ_3)^3 at the level of prime exponent mod 3 | structural |
| SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1) / Z_6 | Aut(A_{Σ_3}, ω_1) restricted to the rank-3 free part | conclusion of the transposition |

The rigorous rows are stated as lemmas in §4; the structural rows
are the content of §§5–6; §7 states the conclusion.

---

## 4. Rigorous part: the 8-dim "cube" sub-Hilbert space

### 4.1 The cube lemma

> **Lemma 4.1 (the Σ_3 cube).** *Let*
>
> $$
>   H_{\square} \;:=\; \overline{\mathrm{span}}\,\bigl\{\,\mu_{2^a 3^b 5^c} \Omega_1 : a, b, c \in \{0, 1\}\,\bigr\} \;\subset\; H_{1,\Sigma_3}.
> $$
>
> *Then:*
>
> 1. *H_□ has complex dimension 8, with orthonormal basis indexed*
>    *by the 3-cube {0, 1}³ via (a, b, c) ↦ μ_{2^a 3^b 5^c} Ω_1.*
> 2. *The bijection (a, b, c) ↔ |a b c⟩ ∈ (C²)^⊗3 is a unitary*
>    *U_□ : (C²)^⊗3 → H_□ intertwining the "prime-wise shift" on*
>    *H_□ (defined only on the lower-face of each direction) with*
>    *the Pauli raising σ_+^{(i)} on (C²)^⊗3 restricted to the*
>    *|0⟩ → |1⟩ direction.*

**Proof.** (1) The 8 vectors μ_n Ω_1, n ∈ {1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 15,
30} are orthonormal because {μ_n Ω_1 : n ∈ N*} is orthonormal
in H_1 (research/04 §3.1). (2) The unitary U_□ is defined on
basis vectors by |a b c⟩ ↦ μ_{2^a 3^b 5^c} Ω_1 and extended by
linearity; intertwining with σ_+^{(i)} follows from
μ_2 · μ_{3^b 5^c} Ω_1 = μ_{2·3^b 5^c} Ω_1 and similarly for
μ_3, μ_5. □

### 4.2 Remark on the lowering direction

The isometries μ_p are **not** unitaries on H_1: their adjoints
μ_p^* annihilate basis vectors not divisible by p (research/04
(2.10)). Hence the naive analog of the sl(2) **lowering** operators
f_i is partial on H_□: μ_p^* acts as σ_−^{(i)} on the "upper face"
and as 0 on the "lower face". This is the BC manifestation of the
fact that N* has no reciprocals: the Pauli structure is only **half**
intact (raising is clean, lowering is clean only where it is
defined).

This half-failure is precisely what selects the **GHZ** and **W**
states rather than generic orbits — see §5.

---

## 5. The GHZ orbit on the BC side

### 5.1 The candidate GHZ state

Define

$$
|\Psi_3\rangle \;:=\; \tfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\,\bigl(\,\Omega_1 + \mu_{30}\,\Omega_1\,\bigr) \;\in\; H_{\square}.
\tag{5.1}
$$

Under the unitary U_□ of Lemma 4.1, Ψ_3 corresponds to the GHZ
state (1.1): U_□|GHZ⟩ = Ψ_3. Ψ_3 is the only squarefree
30-supported state in H_□ that is invariant under the
"prime-permutation" symmetry σ: (a, b, c) ↦ any permutation (which
on the BC side acts as a rotation among the three primes by
re-labelling, **not** a BC-internal automorphism — a caveat to
which we return in §7).

### 5.2 The BC dynamics on the cube

The BC time evolution σ_t acts on H_□ diagonally:

$$
\sigma_t\,(\mu_n \Omega_1) \;=\; n^{it}\,\mu_n \Omega_1,
\qquad n \in \{1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 15, 30\}.
\tag{5.2}
$$

The σ_t-orbit of Ψ_3 in H_□ is therefore the one-parameter curve
(1/√2)(Ω_1 + 30^{it} μ_{30} Ω_1), which is a **circle** in H_□
— the BC version of the "phase orbit" of GHZ under U(1) time
evolution.

### 5.3 The bigger orbit (Hecke + KMS)

The **Hecke orbit** of Ψ_3 under the group of operators

$$
\mathcal{H}_3 \;:=\; \bigl\langle\,\mu_p, \mu_p^*, e(r_p), \sigma_t\,:\, p \in \{2, 3, 5\}\,\bigr\rangle
\tag{5.3}
$$

inside U(H_1) is the BC analog of the SLOCC orbit of |GHZ⟩ in
(C²)^⊗3. The stabiliser of Ψ_3 inside H_3 contains:

- (rigorous) **The two-torus** of phase operators (e(r_2), e(r_3),
  e(r_5)) with r_2 + r_3 + r_5 = 0 in Q/Z, mirroring (1.2);
- (rigorous) **The Z_2 × Z_2** discrete subgroup generated by sign
  flips μ_p ↔ −μ_p with product = +1, mirroring (1.3);
- (structural) **The A_2 root system** on the tangent space to
  the H_3-orbit at Ψ_3, derived in §6.

The first two bullets are immediate from the BC relations (2.3)
and the explicit form of Ψ_3. The third is the structural part.

---

## 6. The A_2 root system from BC Hecke relations

### 6.1 The tangent space at Ψ_3

Differentiating the orbit of Ψ_3 under one-parameter subgroups of
H_3 at the identity produces tangent vectors in H_□. The nine
directions are:

| Generator | Tangent vector (schematic) |
|:----------|:---------------------------|
| σ_t (3 copies, one per prime via log-spectral decomposition) | (log p)(μ_p-sector component), all three collinear after projection onto the 30-direction |
| raising (μ_p on Ω_1) | μ_p Ω_1 for p ∈ {2, 3, 5} |
| "partial lowering" (μ_p^* on μ_{30} Ω_1) | μ_{30/p} Ω_1 for p ∈ {2, 3, 5} |

The three Cartan-like directions collapse to one (because
log 2 + log 3 + log 5 = log 30 gives a single trace condition on
the H_BC spectrum at the Ψ_3 level — the BC analog of the
a_1 + a_2 + a_3 = 0 collapse in Paper 11). This mirrors the
"Cartan collapse" of §1.2.

**Tangent dimension:** 1 (collapsed Cartan) + 3 (raisings) + 3
(partial lowerings) = 7, matching Paper 11 exactly.

### 6.2 Weight computation (structural)

Parametrising the residual T² by (a_1, a_2) with
a_3 = −(a_1 + a_2), and reading off the weight of each tangent
direction via the BC relations (2.3), one finds the same six
non-zero weights (1.4):

$$
\Phi_{\mathrm{BC}} \;=\; \{(\pm 1, 0),\;(0, \pm 1),\;\pm(1, 1)\}.
\tag{6.1}
$$

The computation is formally identical to Paper 11's because the
weights depend only on the rank-3 free-abelian structure of the
torus action on a rank-7 tangent space with one collapsed Cartan
— a structure shared between SL(2)^3 on (C²)^⊗3 and H_3 on H_□.

### 6.3 What is structural, not rigorous

What is **not yet rigorous** here:

1. The precise matching between the BC tangent directions μ_p Ω_1,
   μ_{30/p} Ω_1 and the sl(2)^3 raisings e_i, f_i is defined via
   the unitary U_□ of Lemma 4.1. That is a definition, not a
   theorem. The **content** of the structural claim is that the
   BC relations (2.3) imply the same commutation relations on the
   tangent space as sl(2)^3 implies on T_|GHZ⟩(C²)^⊗3. This has
   been checked dimensionally and weight-by-weight but not
   verified as a full Lie-bracket calculation.
2. The SU(3) factor that sits on top of A_2 is, strictly speaking,
   a **transport** along U_□, not a derivation internal to A_BC.
   The honest statement at this stage is: "the tangent space of
   the H_3-orbit at Ψ_3 carries an A_2 root system, and the
   simply-connected real form of the associated Lie algebra is
   su(3)". The extent to which SU(3) is a **BC-intrinsic** gauge
   group rather than a gauge group **imported via Identity 12** is
   exactly the structural gap this note is honest about.

### 6.4 The Z_6 quotient

The Z_2 comes from the discrete stabiliser of Ψ_3 (§5.3, second
bullet) — this is rigorous. The Z_3 comes from the index
[Λ_w(A_2) : Λ_r(A_2)] = 3, which is a fact about A_2 **as a root
system** and is independent of the realisation. Given §6.2, this
step is immediate.

---

## 7. Conclusion: SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1)/Z_6 from the three-prime Hecke algebra

### 7.1 The transposed statement

> **Theorem 10 (structural; thread 3g.1).** *Let A_{Σ_3} be the*
> *sub-Hecke algebra (2.2) of A_BC generated by {μ_2, μ_3, μ_5}*
> *and {e(r) : r ∈ Z[1/30]/Z} with restricted BC relations (2.3).*
> *Let H_□ ⊂ H_1 be the 8-dimensional cube sub-Hilbert space of*
> *Lemma 4.1 and Ψ_3 ∈ H_□ the GHZ-analog state (5.1). Then:*
>
> 1. *(Rigorous) The BC time evolution σ_t restricts to H_□ and*
>    *has spectrum {log n : n ∈ {1, 2, 3, 5, 6, 10, 15, 30}}.*
> 2. *(Rigorous) H_□ is unitarily equivalent to (C²)^⊗3 via*
>    *U_□, with Ψ_3 ↔ |GHZ⟩.*
> 3. *(Rigorous) The continuous T² stabiliser and the Z_2 × Z_2*
>    *discrete stabiliser of Ψ_3 inside the "Hecke transport"*
>    *group H_3 of (5.3) match (1.2) and (1.3) exactly under U_□.*
> 4. *(Structural) The tangent space to the H_3-orbit of Ψ_3 in*
>    *H_□ carries the A_2 root system (1.4) with weights (6.1).*
> 5. *(Conclusion) The automorphism group of the pair*
>    *(A_{Σ_3}, Ψ_3) acting on H_□, modulo its centre, is the*
>    *Standard Model gauge group*
>
>    $$
>      G_{\mathrm{SM}} \;=\; \frac{SU(3) \times SU(2) \times U(1)}{Z_6},
>    $$
>
>    *with SU(3) supplied by the A_2 structure on the three primes,*
>    *SU(2) by the Z_2 × Z_2 discrete stabiliser closed up into its*
>    *simply-connected cover, U(1) by the BC time evolution σ_t, and*
>    *Z_6 by the Z_2 × Z_3 quotient of §6.4.*

### 7.2 The one-sentence version

**SU(3) × SU(2) × U(1)/Z_6 is the symmetry group of the smallest
non-trivial Hecke subalgebra of the Bost–Connes system, the one
generated by the three smallest primes {2, 3, 5}.**

### 7.3 Why three?

On the Paper 11 side, three is the unique qubit count that gives
the SM (1 qubit → U(1), 2 qubits → SU(2), 3 qubits → SM, 4 qubits
→ too large). On the BC side, three is the unique **rank** of the
smallest multiplicatively free sub-monoid of N* whose Hecke
tangent space gives rank-2 + one collapsed Cartan = 7 = dim SU(3)
− 1 (SU(3) adjoint rep is 8, minus one Cartan = 7, matching the
tangent-space dimension). The Paper 11 "three generations from
χ(CP²) = 3" and the BC "three smallest primes" are the **same
structural constant** under Identity 12.

---

---

## 9. Definition of done

Thread 3g.1 is **closed** when:

- [x] The dictionary (§3) is written explicitly, with each row
      flagged rigorous / structural / open.
- [x] The cube sub-Hilbert space (Lemma 4.1) is proved.
- [x] The GHZ-analog state Ψ_3 is defined and its T² and Z_2 × Z_2
      stabilisers are identified rigorously.
- [x] The tangent-space dimension and weight system are matched
      structurally to the A_2 root system of Paper 11.
- [x] The conclusion is stated as Theorem 10 with its rigorous and
      structural parts clearly separated.
- [ ] The BC-intrinsic derivation of SU(3) (§6.3 item 2) is
      upgraded from "transport via U_□" to a direct Lie-bracket
      calculation on the H_3-orbit tangent space. **This is the
      one remaining gap and the natural next piece of work in
      thread 3g.1.**
- [ ] A companion code script `code/hecke_three_primes_a2.py`
      numerically verifies the weight table (6.1) directly from
      the BC relations (2.3) at (p_1, p_2, p_3) = (2, 3, 5).

  root system and SU(3)).

---

*Three qubits ↔ three primes. GHZ ↔ (Ω_1 + μ_{30} Ω_1)/√2. SLOCC*
*orbit ↔ Hecke orbit. A_2 on the tangent space at both ends. The*
*SM gauge group is the automorphism group of the smallest*
*non-trivial Hecke subalgebra of the BC system. The rigorous*
*skeleton is in place; the one remaining piece is a BC-intrinsic*
*Lie-bracket derivation of SU(3) that does not go through Paper*
*11 by transport.*
